mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals easing financial conditions, reducing the discount rate on equities and supporting S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts do not coincide with better forward returns than unchanged or rising policy rates.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher in months with falling FEDFUNDS than in months with flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, labor-market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession risk, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when unemployment rises but equities continue to price a soft landing.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses significance after accounting for the prior 3-month trend in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, which should lift near-term corporate revenue expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism fails if stronger labor and sales data do not translate into immediate equity strength.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS signal has no incremental predictive power over either series alone, or if 5d forward returns are not positive in the top signal quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO with flat or falling CPIAUCSL implies output growth without inflation pressure, which should extend equity multiple expansion and support S&P 500 gains over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if higher industrial production is accompanied by accelerating inflation.
failure_condition: Rejected if the sign flips when CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates, or if 60d forward SPX returns are not above baseline when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation shock in CPIAUCSL above its recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation rate implies negative real policy rates and rising odds of future tightening, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails if markets have already fully priced the inflation surprise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS stays below CPIAUCSL inflation
failure_condition: Rejected if inflation-surprise episodes do not deliver negative 20d SPX returns, or if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS signal is not stronger than CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST indicates housing demand and construction activity are weakening, which typically leads cyclical growth expectations lower and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when housing weakness is isolated and does not spread to broader demand measures.
failure_condition: Rejected if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the signal's rolling hit rate stays near random over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is losing jobs and slack is building, which should pressure earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if payroll weakness is offset by falling participation or transitory seasonal noise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines over 2 months and UNRATE rises over the same window
failure_condition: Rejected if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered periods fail to show negative 20d returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 alongside improving RSAFS marks re-acceleration in final demand and should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if GDP growth improves without a matching retail-sales recovery.
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with improving GDPC1 and RSAFS do not show above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value beyond GDPC1 alone.
